# paren-fuzz known-mismatch allowlist.
#
# Format: one mismatch per line, three pipe-separated fields:
#   <sql> | <pg_status> | <omni_status>
#
# pg_status:   "accept" or "reject(42601)"
# omni_status: "subquery" | "joined_table" | "other" | "reject"
#
# The fuzz test normalizes discovered mismatches to this same triple
# and requires set-equality with this file: any newly discovered
# mismatch fails the test (regression); any expected mismatch that
# disappears from the discovered set also fails (drives re-baseline).
#
# Blank lines and lines starting with `#` are ignored.
#
# Current entries = residual known bugs (all tracked in
# pg/parser/PAREN_KNOWN_BUGS.md):
#   PAREN-KB-1 — inner JOIN accepted with no ON/USING
#
# Trailing-SELECT cases (PAREN-KB-2) were previously in the corpus but
# were removed — they measure a top-level statement-list bug, not a `(`
# dispatch question. See PAREN_KNOWN_BUGS.md for the full rationale.

SELECT * FROM (T JOIN U) | reject(42601) | joined_table
SELECT * FROM (T LEFT JOIN T) | reject(42601) | joined_table
SELECT * FROM (foo LEFT JOIN W) | reject(42601) | joined_table
SELECT * FROM (foo RIGHT OUTER JOIN W) | reject(42601) | joined_table
